text:
There once was a beautiful princess who lived in a beautiful palace. Her greatest desire was to learn how to fly. She did everything she could: listened to the wise old sage who said she could only find true love lifting feathers from a peacock. She dreamt of soaring through azure skies.

One day, while wandering through a moonlit meadow, a handsome prince flying a phoenix-feather feather glowed nearby. They chased each other for hours, their laughter echoing through the meadow. Eventually, they flew through a magical valley where flowers sang sweetly. That's where Prince Celeste discovered the Secret Swan Feather, said to grant flight—but only once.

The next morning, Celeste couldn't wait. But as she touched it, feathers shimmered back into a beautiful singing bird. He swooped down and took her feet into his beak. She felt a rush. They flew around the enchanted forest, seeing things no one ever saw.

When they flew too high, the feathers fell
